understanding.goal_understanding: The goal is to complete all levels by moving every selectable piece onto a goal tile (marked '+' in the semantic grid) such that each goal is occupied by a piece simultaneously. The game declares WIN when this condition is met for the current level, and winning all levels completes the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each cell is 3 pixels. MOVE actions shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, unless blocked by walls or other immovable objects. Some objects (blocks) may be pushable. CLICK is used to switch control to another selectable piece at the specified pixel coordinates; it only succeeds if there is a selectable piece at that exact position. CLICK does not move the selected piece. The `movement.{direction}_blocked` hint indicates an adjacent object but does not guarantee a move will fail. The semantic grid shows P for the currently selected piece, S for other selectables, B for blocks, # for walls, + for goals. Positions are given as [x, y] pixel coordinates. Each level has a limited step budget, so actions must be used efficiently.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal is to complete all levels by moving every selectable piece onto a goal tile (marked '+' in the semantic grid) such that each goal is occupied by a piece simultaneously. The game declares WIN when this condition is met for the current level, and winning all levels completes the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each cell is 3 pixels. MOVE actions shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, unless blocked by walls or other immovable objects. Some objects (blocks) may be pushable. CLICK is used to switch control to another selectable piece at the specified pixel coordinates; it only succeeds if there is a selectable piece at that exact position. CLICK does not move the selected piece. The `movement.{direction}_blocked` hint indicates an adjacent object but does not guarantee a move will fail. The semantic grid shows P for the currently selected piece, S for other selectables, B for blocks, # for walls, + for goals. Positions are given as [x, y] pixel coordinates. Each level has a limited step budget, so actions must be used efficiently.
